Audit Summary.Control / Item: Right to erasure (partial operational path exists): user deletion workflow removes user record plus related tokens/requests in course-forge-backend/src/main/java/com/mundrisoft/courseforge/service/UserService.java (deleteUser).

Personal data collection is explicit through DTO contracts with validation annotations (e.g., UserRequestDto, UpdateBillingContactRequestDto, SetPasswordDto) under course-forge-backend/src/main/java/com/mundrisoft/courseforge/dto.
Some retention logic exists for temporary/operational files and editor sessions via scheduled cleanup in FileCleanupService and SCORM session cleanup services.

Data portability is implemented for course/package exports (course-forge-backend/src/main/java/com/mundrisoft/courseforge/export/controller/CourseExportController.java) but not clearly for subject-level personal data export.
Data minimization is inconsistent: DTO validation exists, but broad collection of contact/profile fields is present across multiple DTOs without code-level purpose/necessity enforcement.
Purpose limitation is partly implicit in service/controller flows, but there is no explicit code-level tagging/enforcement of processing purposes.

Data inventory and access mapping are partially covered through DTO/entity/service usage, but there is no centralized record of what personal data is held, where stored, and who can access each category.
Data retention exists in isolated flows (file/session cleanup, user deletion path) but not as a complete GDPR retention schedule per data category.

Security controls are partially implemented (JWT auth, BCrypt, selective audit logging), but GDPR-specific safeguards such as pseudonymization/anonymization policy enforcement are not uniformly applied.

Email consent-related mechanics exist in auth/verification flows, but explicit GDPR-grade marketing consent state and unsubscribe lifecycle governance are not fully modeled in current backend artifacts.
Consent tracking controls are not evidenced in code (no clear consent entity/ledger/versioned consent capture flow found).
Comprehensive GDPR subject-access/export endpoint for user/account/billing activity data is not evidenced.
Centralized retention/deletion policy engine for persisted PII across all entities is not evidenced.
No explicit minors/age-threshold (under 16) collection and parental consent control flow is evidenced in backend privacy controls.
No dedicated record of processing activities (RoPA-style) is evidenced for GDPR accountability requirements.
No explicit third-party processor governance register (processor list, purpose, DPA status, controls) is evidenced in code-level compliance assets.
No international transfer control register (EEA to non-EEA transfer basis, safeguards, risk assessment references) is evidenced in compliance artifacts.
Cookie-consent management backend support (opt-in categories, persistent opt-out, consent recall/change history) is not evidenced in current backend scope.
Form-level consent metadata capture (privacy notice acceptance, correspondence opt-in choices, timestamped consent proof) is not comprehensively modeled across data collection endpoints.

Breach-response readiness controls (72-hour supervisory notification workflow, affected-user notification workflow, incident evidence package) are not implemented as structured backend process modules.
Privacy policy version acceptance tracking (which user accepted which policy version and when) is not evidenced as a dedicated compliance control.
Authentication token validation exists via JWT utility and interceptor (JwtUtil, JwtInterceptor, WebConfig).
Password hashing with BCrypt is configured and used (SecurityConfig password encoder; usage in auth/user service flows).
Audit event mechanism exists (UserAccessEvent + AuditTrailListener) and at least one controller publishes audit events (UserController).
Basic health monitoring exposure exists through Actuator health endpoint in application.properties and application-prod.properties.
Access control is split/inconsistent: Spring Security filter chains permit all (SecurityConfig), while auth enforcement is mainly custom interceptor/manual checks.
RBAC exists but is uneven: isolated @PreAuthorize and manual role checks in controllers/services, not uniformly enforced through a centralized policy model.
Audit logging coverage appears limited to selected actions; no repository-wide evidence of comprehensive security/audit trail for all critical data changes.
Evidence of Redis caching controls is missing because Redis integration itself is not present (no Redis client/config usage found).
Evidence of Riak storage controls is missing because Riak integration itself is not present (no Riak usage found).
Centralized security monitoring instrumentation (e.g., broad metrics/tracing/alert hooks) is not clearly evidenced beyond health endpoint and application logs.
Password protection control is implemented with BCrypt (SecurityConfig + auth/user service encoding calls).
JWT signatures are cryptographically validated using HS256 in JwtUtil.
TLS usage is configured for SMTP (smtp.ssl.enabled=true, TLS protocol settings in application property files).
Encryption practice is mixed: strong password hashing exists, but symmetric utility uses hardcoded key and AES/ECB mode in EncryptionUtil.
Secure configuration is partial: production profile reduces verbosity and limits actuator exposure, but insecure/unsafe defaults remain in config set (e.g., DB useSSL=false, ddl-auto=update).
Risk controls are partially documented in code comments and selective checks, but not uniformly codified across all sensitive flows.
Secrets management is not implemented in a compliant code pattern: numerous secrets/credentials/API keys are directly present in application.properties and application-prod.properties.
No code evidence of key rotation/revocation lifecycle for JWT secrets/API secrets.
No code evidence of systematic field-level encryption policy for sensitive persisted business/PII data.
Risk Item
Audit Summary.Risk (if applicable): Hardcoded secrets and credentials in config files (spring.datasource.password, jwt.secret, OpenAI keys, SMTP password, ZeptoMail key, Stripe keys) create credential exposure and compromise risk.

Audit Summary.Control / Item: Risk Item
Audit Summary.Risk (if applicable): Authorization model risk: SecurityConfig permits all requests at filter-chain level, with enforcement moved to custom interceptor/manual checks, increasing bypass/misconfiguration risk.

Audit Summary.Control / Item: Risk Item
Audit Summary.Risk (if applicable): Potential sensitive-data logging risk: request-associated values (e.g., imageUrl, sessionId, search criteria) and exception message propagation appear in controller logging/response paths.

Audit Summary.Control / Item: Risk Item
Audit Summary.Risk (if applicable): Cryptographic weakness risk from EncryptionUtil hardcoded AES key and AES/ECB/PKCS5Padding usage.

Audit Summary.Control / Item: Risk Item
Audit Summary.Risk (if applicable): Data subject rights coverage risk: deletion exists for user path, but comprehensive personal-data portability and consent lifecycle evidence is not present.

Audit Summary.Control / Item: Risk Item
Audit Summary.Risk (if applicable): Assurance gap risk for cache/NoSQL controls because Redis/Riak control implementation is absent in code.

Audit Summary.Control / Item: Risk Item
Audit Summary.Risk (if applicable): GDPR governance risk: missing centralized processor/international transfer controls can expose the organization to cross-border compliance violations.

Audit Summary.Control / Item: Risk Item
Audit Summary.Risk (if applicable): Consent evidence risk: absence of structured cookie/form/privacy-policy consent logs may weaken legal defensibility during audits or complaints.

Audit Summary.Control / Item: Risk Item
Audit Summary.Risk (if applicable): Incident response risk: without breach workflow orchestration and notification tracking, 72-hour GDPR notification obligations may be missed.

Audit Summary.Control / Item: Requires infrastructure or policy validation: whether repository secrets are overridden by runtime secret managers and not used in deployed environments.
Requires infrastructure or policy validation: production network controls around actuator and API endpoints (private access, WAF/API gateway rules).
Requires infrastructure or policy validation: log pipeline encryption, retention, RBAC, redaction, and SIEM forwarding controls.
Requires infrastructure or policy validation: database/storage encryption at rest, backup encryption, and KMS/HSM governance.
Requires infrastructure or policy validation: formal consent governance/process may exist outside application code (legal/policy/workflow systems).
Requires infrastructure or policy validation: incident monitoring/alerting stack may exist outside this repository.
Requires infrastructure or policy validation: whether a separate legal/compliance system maintains RoPA, processor DPAs, and transfer impact assessments outside this repository.
Requires infrastructure or policy validation: whether frontend/web platform already implements GDPR cookie banner mechanics with backend consent persistence not represented here.
Requires infrastructure or policy validation: whether formal incident response runbooks and regulatory notification workflows are implemented in SOC/ITSM tools external to codebase.

Design Plan.Issue: GDPR Partial: Data portability only covers course/package exports, not subject-level personal data export
Design Plan.Root Cause: Export implementation is domain-specific (CourseExportController/export services) and there is no unified data-subject aggregation service spanning user, billing, auth, and usage repositories.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Add a GDPR portability module with DataPortabilityController (GET /v1/privacy/me/export), DataPortabilityService, and repository adapters. Use async export orchestration (job table + signed download URL) and DTO-based s…
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Controller: new DataPortabilityController secured with @PreAuthorize("isAuthenticated()"); Service: DataPortabilityService.buildExport(userId) pulls from UserRepository, BillingContactRepository, ApiUsageRepository, etc…
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: add privacy_export_jobs (id, user_id, status, expires_at, checksum); Redis: cache export job progress with TTL (e.g., 24h) to avoid polling DB hot paths; Riak: optional object bucket for large export artifact chunks…
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Require fresh JWT + optional step-up check before export generation, sign download links, enforce one-user access boundary, and audit each export request/result.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR Art. 20 (Data Portability), SOC2 CC6/CC7 (access and processing control), ISO 27001 A.5/A.8 (information access and handling)

Design Plan.Issue: GDPR Partial: Data minimization is inconsistent across DTOs
Design Plan.Root Cause: DTOs validate format but not necessity; controllers accept broad payloads without purpose-scoped field policies, and services do not enforce context-aware field whitelists.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Introduce purpose-scoped request models and validation groups (@Validated(CreateProfile.class) etc.). Add a DataMinimizationPolicyService enforcing allowed fields per endpoint purpose and role.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Controller: replace generic DTOs with purpose-specific DTOs (BillingContactUpdateDto, TenantOnboardingDto, etc.); Service: add validateAllowedFields(purpose, payload) before persistence; Config: add custom ConstraintVal…
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: no mandatory schema change, but optionally split rarely used sensitive attributes into separate table with strict access path; Redis: cache minimization policy map keyed by endpoint+role with short TTL.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Reduce attack surface from over-posting/mass assignment, enforce strict JSON property binding (FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES=true for sensitive endpoints), and reject unnecessary PII ingestion.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) Data Minimization, SOC2 CC3/CC6, ISO 27001 A.8 (information classification/handling)

Design Plan.Issue: GDPR Partial: Purpose limitation is implicit, not explicit/enforced
Design Plan.Root Cause: Processing purpose is embedded in business logic and not represented as a first-class policy attribute in API/service layers.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Add purpose-tag propagation from API to service (ProcessingPurpose enum). Enforce via AOP interceptor (@RequiresPurpose) and policy matrix service.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Controller: annotate endpoints with @RequiresPurpose(Purpose.BILLING_CONTACT_UPDATE); Service: accept ProcessingContext with purpose and actor; Aspect: block unauthorized purpose/data combinations before method executio…
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: add processing_purpose and legal_basis columns to audit records; Redis: cache purpose policy matrix and invalidate on policy change; Riak: include purpose metadata in object headers for immutable audit context.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Centralized purpose checks prevent function creep, improve traceability, and harden against unauthorized secondary use of collected data.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR Art. 5(1)(b) Purpose Limitation, SOC2 CC2/CC7, ISO 27001 A.5/A.8

Design Plan.Issue: GDPR Missing: Consent tracking ledger/versioning not implemented
Design Plan.Root Cause: No consent domain model, no evented consent capture endpoint, and no versioned policy references tied to user actions.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Implement ConsentController + ConsentService with versioned consent records (policy_version, purpose, status, timestamp, source_ip, user_agent). Use append-only consent events and immutable hash chain for integrity.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Controller: POST /v1/privacy/consents and GET /v1/privacy/consents; Service: recordConsent(command) and withdrawConsent(...); DAO: ConsentRepository; add @PreAuthorize("isAuthenticated()") and service-level ownership ch…
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: new user_consents and consent_events tables with unique index (user_id,purpose,policy_version); Redis: cache current consent state per user/purpose TTL 15m for fast policy checks; Riak: store consent event snapshots…
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Sign consent payload digest, enforce write-once event records, and include consent status check in business flows before processing optional personal data.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR Art. 6/7 (lawful basis and consent), SOC2 CC6/CC8, ISO 27001 A.5/A.8/A.12

Design Plan.Issue: GDPR Missing: Comprehensive subject-access endpoint not implemented
Design Plan.Root Cause: No DSAR orchestration layer; data access remains fragmented by bounded contexts and endpoint ownership.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Add DSAR APIs (/v1/privacy/me/access-report) producing machine-readable + human-readable reports. Orchestrate with service fan-out and standardized mappers.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Controller: DataSubjectAccessController; Service: DsarAggregationService; DAO: repository adapters returning scoped projections; add request throttling and asynchronous job processing.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: dsar_requests table for status, SLA clock, and fulfillment logs; Redis: DSAR workflow state TTL and idempotency keys; Riak: archive generated reports with retention policy metadata.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Strong requester verification, request anti-replay, per-user scoping, signed report artifacts, and complete DSAR audit trail.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR Art. 15 (Right of Access), SOC2 CC6/CC7, ISO 27001 A.5/A.8

Design Plan.Issue: GDPR Missing: Centralized retention/deletion policy engine for PII
Design Plan.Root Cause: Retention is implemented in isolated services (file/session cleanup) without global policy registry and entity-level enforcement.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Implement retention policy engine with declarative rules (RetentionPolicy), scheduled executor, and deletion/anonymization handlers per entity type.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Config: RetentionPolicyConfig loads policy from DB/YAML; Service: RetentionEngineService.run(); DAO: retention queries per repository; add @Scheduled orchestrator + dead-letter handling.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: retention_policies and retention_execution_log; Redis: lock keys (SETNX) for singleton scheduler and per-batch checkpoints TTL; Riak: use bucket-level lifecycle tags and delete markers.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Deterministic expiry handling, reduced stale-PII risk, tamper-evident execution logs, and controlled purge workflows with dual-control for sensitive domains.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) Storage Limitation & Art. 17, SOC2 CC8, ISO 27001 A.8/A.12

Design Plan.Issue: SOC2 Partial: Access control split between permitAll filter chain and custom interceptor/manual checks
Design Plan.Root Cause: Spring Security is not the single enforcement point; authorization logic is duplicated in interceptors/controllers, creating policy drift.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Move to SecurityFilterChain-first enforcement: deny-by-default, JWT auth filter (OncePerRequestFilter), and standardized exception handling (AuthenticationEntryPoint, AccessDeniedHandler).
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Config: replace .anyRequest().permitAll() with explicit permit list + .anyRequest().authenticated(); Security: add JwtAuthenticationFilter populating SecurityContext; Controller/Service: remove manual bearer parsing and…
Design Plan.Data Changes: Redis: maintain token jti blacklist/session-state cache TTL to support revocation and logout; DB optional: revoked_tokens for durable revocation.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Single policy plane, fewer bypass paths, consistent 401/403 behavior, and stricter endpoint hardening.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: SOC2 CC6 (logical access), ISO 27001 A.5/A.8, GDPR Art. 32

Design Plan.Issue: SOC2 Partial: RBAC exists but is uneven and not centralized
Design Plan.Root Cause: Role checks are mixed (@PreAuthorize in few places, manual role parsing elsewhere) and no unified authorization policy service exists.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Enable method security globally and standardize role/attribute checks with SpEL policies + custom PermissionEvaluator.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Config: @EnableMethodSecurity; Service/Controller: annotate privileged operations (@PreAuthorize("hasRole('SUPER_ADMIN') or @perm.canAccessWorkspace(authentication,#workspaceId)")); add PermissionEvaluator and workspace…
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: normalize role-permission matrix tables (roles, permissions, role_permissions); Redis: cache permission graph by role with cache invalidation on admin changes.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Consistent least-privilege authorization, context-aware access checks, and reduction of ad-hoc role parsing bugs.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: SOC2 CC6.1/CC6.2, ISO 27001 A.5 (access control), GDPR Art. 32

Design Plan.Issue: SOC2 Partial: Audit logging coverage is limited to selected actions
Design Plan.Root Cause: Audit event publication is manual and sparse; no cross-cutting mechanism captures high-risk CRUD/auth/privilege actions consistently.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Implement audit as cross-cutting concern using AOP (@Auditable) plus security event listeners and persistence-backed audit store.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Create @Auditable(action="..."); Aspect captures actor, target, before/after hash, request ID; Service layer annotates critical methods; add async AuditWriter with SLF4J structured JSON output.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: audit_log table partitioned by date; Redis: short-term stream/buffer for burst writes; Riak: immutable archive bucket for long-retention audit records.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Tamper-resistant logs (hash chain/signature), full traceability for sensitive operations, and easier incident reconstruction.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: SOC2 CC7/CC8, ISO 27001 A.12 (logging/monitoring), GDPR Art. 30/32

Design Plan.Issue: SOC2 Missing: Redis caching controls absent because Redis integration is absent
Design Plan.Root Cause: No cache architecture was implemented for security-sensitive state (revocation, rate limiting, ephemeral risk controls).
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Add Redis with Spring Data Redis for token revocation, rate limiting, and ephemeral policy caches. Define cache namespaces and security TTL standards.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Config: RedisConfig (LettuceConnectionFactory, RedisTemplate, cache manager); Security: TokenRevocationService using jti keys; Filter: rate-limit filter reading Redis counters.
Design Plan.Data Changes: Redis keys: auth:revoked:{jti} TTL=token-exp, rl:login:{ip} sliding window TTL, policy:rbac:{role} TTL=15m; DB remains source of truth; Riak unaffected for this control.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Immediate revocation capability, brute-force mitigation, reduced stale authorization decisions.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: SOC2 CC6/CC7, ISO 27001 A.8/A.12, GDPR Art. 32

Design Plan.Issue: SOC2 Missing: Riak storage controls absent because Riak integration is absent
Design Plan.Root Cause: No object-storage strategy exists for immutable security/compliance artifacts (audit snapshots, DSAR archives, consent snapshots).
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Introduce Riak adapter layer for append-only compliance artifacts with bucket types enforcing replication and conflict policies.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Create RiakClientConfig and RiakComplianceStore service; write through service APIs only (storeAuditSnapshot, storeExportArtifact); maintain repository abstraction to avoid controller-level storage calls.
Design Plan.Data Changes: Riak buckets: audit-archive, privacy-export, consent-snapshot; store encrypted payload + metadata (owner, purpose, retention_until); Redis caches object pointers for fast retrieval; DB stores index/reference records.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Durable immutable archival path, separation of operational DB from compliance evidence, and safer long-term artifact retention.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: SOC2 CC8/CC9, ISO 27001 A.8/A.12, GDPR Art. 30/32

Design Plan.Issue: SOC2 Missing: Centralized monitoring/tracing instrumentation not implemented
Design Plan.Root Cause: Monitoring is mostly health endpoint + app logs; no standardized metrics, tracing, or alert-grade security telemetry emission.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Adopt Micrometer + Spring Boot Actuator metrics and structured security telemetry pipeline. Add security KPIs and incident-oriented events.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Config: expose metrics endpoint internally; Service/Filter: publish counters (auth_failures, token_revocations, access_denied); add HandlerInterceptor for latency/status tagging and correlation-id propagation.
Design Plan.Data Changes: Redis: optional rolling counters for near-real-time anomaly checks; Riak: store periodic immutable monitoring snapshots for audit evidence; DB: optional security_incidents table.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Faster detection of abuse/misconfiguration, measurable control effectiveness, and better forensic readiness.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: SOC2 CC7 (monitoring), ISO 27001 A.12/A.16, GDPR Art. 32

Design Plan.Issue: ISO27001 Partial: Encryption practice is mixed (BCrypt good, AES utility weak)
Design Plan.Root Cause: Symmetric crypto utility uses hardcoded key and ECB mode, likely introduced as convenience without centralized crypto policy.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Replace with envelope encryption using AES-GCM (Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding")) and key material from KMS/Vault. Standardize via CryptoService bean.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Service: CryptoService.encrypt/decrypt with random IV per record and auth tag; Config: inject active key id from env/secret manager; replace direct EncryptionUtil calls in auth/user services.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB/Riak: store {keyId, iv, ciphertext, tag} per encrypted field/object; Redis: never store plaintext secrets; cache only encrypted blobs or opaque references with TTL.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Confidentiality + integrity protection, removal of static key exposure, and cryptographic agility via key ids.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: ISO 27001 A.8/A.10, SOC2 CC6.6, GDPR Art. 32

Design Plan.Issue: ISO27001 Partial: Secure configuration has unsafe defaults (useSSL=false, ddl-auto=update)
Design Plan.Root Cause: Environment hardening boundaries are not enforced by profile-specific config governance and startup validation.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Implement secure-by-default profile policy with startup guard rails (@ConfigurationProperties + validator) that fail fast on unsafe production settings.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Config: SecurityBaselineProperties + @PostConstruct checks (if(prod && !dbSsl) throw); Security config: tighten actuator and CORS defaults; remove dangerous fallbacks from prod properties.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: enforce TLS connection parameters and cert validation; Redis/Riak clients configured with TLS and auth in production profiles.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Prevents insecure runtime drift, ensures encrypted transport to data stores, and blocks accidental weak deployments.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: ISO 27001 A.5/A.8/A.12, SOC2 CC5/CC6, GDPR Art. 32

Design Plan.Issue: ISO27001 Partial: Risk controls are selective, not codified uniformly
Design Plan.Root Cause: Security controls are spread across ad-hoc checks and comments; no policy-as-code baseline across modules.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Create security control framework: baseline annotations, centralized validators, and CI policy checks for critical controls.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Add @SensitiveOperation + interceptor enforcing MFA freshness/role checks where required; add centralized SecurityPolicyService; add build-time checks (e.g., ArchUnit) enforcing controller/service security rules.
Design Plan.Data Changes: Redis: store control state (e.g., risk flags/session trust level) with TTL; Riak: archive periodic policy compliance snapshots.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Consistent risk treatment, fewer control gaps in new modules, and auditable enforcement semantics.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: ISO 27001 A.5/A.6/A.12, SOC2 CC3/CC6/CC8

Design Plan.Issue: ISO27001 Missing: Secrets management pattern not compliant (secrets in properties)
Design Plan.Root Cause: Application reads static secrets from repo-managed property files instead of runtime secret sources.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Externalize all secrets to Vault/KMS/secret manager and inject via environment or Spring Cloud Vault; keep properties as placeholders only.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Config: replace literal secrets with ${...} references; add SecretProvider abstraction for JWT/DB/API keys; startup check rejects default/test secret values in non-dev profiles.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: rotate credentials and move to short-lived auth where possible; Redis/Riak credentials sourced from secret manager and rotated regularly.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Eliminates credential leakage from SCM, supports rotation, and reduces blast radius of compromised artifacts.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: ISO 27001 A.5/A.8, SOC2 CC6.1/CC6.6, GDPR Art. 32

Design Plan.Issue: ISO27001 Missing: No key rotation/revocation lifecycle for JWT/API secrets
Design Plan.Root Cause: Single static key model is used; JWT validation does not support multi-key (kid) or revocation lifecycle.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Implement key management lifecycle: JWKS-style key ring, active/previous keys, scheduled rotation, and emergency revocation.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Security: add JwtKeyProvider supporting kid; token minting adds header kid; validation resolves key by kid; add admin/internal endpoint to mark keys revoked and trigger cache invalidation.
Design Plan.Data Changes: Redis: keys:active, keys:revoked:{kid} with TTL; DB: security_keys table (kid, status, created_at, revoked_at); Riak: immutable key-rotation audit records.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Supports seamless rotation, fast revocation after compromise, and reduced long-term key exposure risk.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: ISO 27001 A.10/A.12, SOC2 CC6/CC7, GDPR Art. 32

Design Plan.Issue: ISO27001 Missing: No systematic field-level encryption for sensitive persisted data
Design Plan.Root Cause: PII is stored in plaintext in operational entities; encryption is not enforced by persistence layer policy.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Apply transparent field encryption with JPA AttributeConverter or service-level crypto wrappers for marked fields (@EncryptedField).
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Entity/DAO: annotate sensitive fields (email/phone/address/tokens) and bind converters; Service: decrypt only at boundary where needed; add masking in DTO mappers for non-privileged responses.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: migrate existing plaintext to encrypted columns via controlled migration job; Redis: store only masked/minimal PII; Riak: encrypt object payload before write and maintain keyId metadata.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Data-at-rest hardening for PII, reduced impact from DB dumps, and stronger compartmentalization of sensitive values.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: ISO 27001 A.8/A.10, SOC2 CC6.6, GDPR Art. 32

Design Plan.Issue: Cross-cutting Risk: Sensitive data appears in logs and exception messages
Design Plan.Root Cause: Controllers log request-associated values and concatenate raw exception messages into API responses; no centralized redaction policy.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Adopt structured logging with redaction filters and standardized error translation (@ControllerAdvice) returning sanitized error codes.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Logging: add MaskingPatternLayout/MDC redaction strategy; Web: global exception handler maps internal exceptions to safe payloads; remove direct logging of URLs/session IDs/PII fields in controllers.
Design Plan.Data Changes: Redis: optional temporary correlation-id map for incident tracing without storing raw payload; Riak: long-term audit logs store masked fields only.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Prevents PII/token leakage via logs/responses, improves incident handling without exposing sensitive context.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR Art. 5(1)(f)/32, SOC2 CC6/CC7, ISO 27001 A.12

Design Plan.Issue: GDPR Missing: Personal data inventory and access registry is not centralized
Design Plan.Root Cause: Personal data usage is spread across DTOs/entities/services without a single inventory mapping data category, storage location, access scope, and processor context.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Implement a Data Inventory Registry module that maps personal/sensitive data fields to source endpoints, storage systems, retention period, and authorized roles; keep registry queryable for audits.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Add DataInventoryController (internal/compliance), DataInventoryService, and metadata scanners for DTO/entity annotations; add role-restricted endpoints to view inventory and access map.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: data_inventory_catalog table with fields for data_type, sensitivity, purpose, storage_system, retention_rule, access_roles, processor_reference; Redis optional cache for lookup.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Improves accountability, least-privilege reviews, and rapid impact analysis during incidents or data-subject requests.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR accountability/Art. 30 readiness, SOC2 CC3/CC6, ISO 27001 A.5/A.8

Design Plan.Issue: GDPR Missing: Minors (under 16) consent and age-gating controls are not implemented
Design Plan.Root Cause: Registration/data collection flows do not model age verification or parental authorization conditions.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Add age-threshold validation and parental consent workflow for minors, including status transitions and verification evidence retention.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Update relevant registration/profile DTOs and controllers with age fields/flags; add MinorConsentService and guard checks before processing optional personal data.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: user_age_verification and parental_consent_records tables; Redis for pending verification/session TTL.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Reduces unlawful processing risk for minors and enforces stricter controls over sensitive user segments.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR Art. 8 (children consent), SOC2 CC6, ISO 27001 A.8

Design Plan.Issue: GDPR Missing: Cookie and form consent lifecycle controls are not comprehensively implemented
Design Plan.Root Cause: Consent capture is not standardized for cookie categories, form privacy notices, correspondence opt-in, and consent withdrawal history.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Implement Consent Preference Center backend APIs for cookie categories, form consent proof, correspondence preferences, and withdrawal/change history.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Add ConsentPreferenceController and FormConsentService; enforce consent checks in data-collection endpoints and messaging workflows.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: consent_preferences, form_consent_events, correspondence_preferences; Redis for active consent cache with durable DB source.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Creates auditable proof of consent state and persistent opt-out enforcement.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR consent transparency/opt-in expectations, SOC2 CC7/CC8, ISO 27001 A.12

Design Plan.Issue: GDPR Missing: Third-party processor and international transfer governance is not modeled
Design Plan.Root Cause: Backend compliance artifacts do not maintain processor catalog, transfer basis, safeguards, or review status.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Add Processor Governance module to register third-party processors, transfer regions, legal basis, DPA status, and transfer safeguards.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Add ProcessorController and TransferGovernanceService with policy validation hooks for integrations that handle personal data.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: processors, processor_assessments, transfer_mechanisms, transfer_risk_reviews tables; optional Riak archival of signed assessment documents.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Strengthens vendor oversight and cross-border transfer accountability.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR Chapter V transfer governance, SOC2 CC3/CC9, ISO 27001 supplier relationship controls

Design Plan.Issue: GDPR Missing: Breach response and 72-hour notification workflow is not implemented
Design Plan.Root Cause: No structured incident workflow exists for breach classification, authority notification timelines, affected-user communication, and corrective action tracking.
Design Plan.Solution Summary: Implement Breach Response Orchestration module with incident states, timer-based SLA checks, notification templates, and evidence bundle generation.
Design Plan.Code Changes (short): Add BreachIncidentController and BreachResponseService; integrate security event triggers and escalation hooks.
Design Plan.Data Changes: DB: breach_incidents, breach_notifications, breach_actions, breach_impacted_subjects tables; Redis timer queues for SLA reminders.
Design Plan.Security Improvements: Improves regulatory response readiness and repeatable incident handling.
Design Plan.Compliance Mapping: GDPR Art. 33/34, SOC2 CC7/CC8, ISO 27001 incident management controls

Estimation.Issue: GDPR Partial: Data portability only covers course/package exports, not subject-level personal data export
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Design `PersonalDataExportDto` contract and export schema (JSON + optional CSV bundle), Build `DataPortabilityController` and async export job APIs, Implement `DataPortabilityService` with fan-out across user/billing/auth/usage repositories, Add export job persistence, status polling, and signed download URL flow, Add audit events for request/start/complete/download actions, Add integration tests for ownership, payload completeness, and expiry behavior
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Cross-domain data aggregation and async orchestration are required; backward compatibility must be preserved while introducing new privacy APIs and download workflow.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO, Redis, Riak, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: Export artifact storage decision (DB filesystem vs Riak), Token/signing strategy for download links, Infra capacity for large export generation

Estimation.Issue: GDPR Partial: Data minimization is inconsistent across DTOs
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Inventory current DTOs and classify fields by legal/business necessity, Create purpose-scoped DTO variants and validation groups, Implement `DataMinimizationPolicyService` for endpoint-role field allowlists, Harden JSON binding for sensitive endpoints (reject unknown/excess fields), Refactor affected controllers/services to new DTO contracts, Add regression tests for over-posting and blocked fields
Estimation.Complexity Reason: High controller/service touch points and high regression risk due to request-contract changes across many endpoints.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: Product/legal sign-off on minimum data set per endpoint, API contract compatibility plan for existing clients

Estimation.Issue: GDPR Partial: Purpose limitation is implicit, not explicit/enforced
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Define `ProcessingPurpose` enum and purpose-policy matrix, Implement `@RequiresPurpose` annotation and AOP enforcement, Propagate processing context from controller to service methods, Add audit metadata (`purpose`, `legal_basis`) to trail records, Add policy caching and invalidation flow
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Requires cross-cutting pattern changes and method signature updates in service layer; impacts many existing flows.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO, Redis, Riak, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: Policy ownership model (who defines and updates purpose matrix), Coordination with audit logging model

Estimation.Issue: GDPR Missing: Consent tracking ledger/versioning not implemented
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Design consent domain model (`user_consents`, `consent_events`), Implement `ConsentController` for capture/read/withdraw endpoints, Implement append-only consent event write path with integrity hash, Add consent state cache for runtime policy checks, Integrate consent checks into optional-data processing services, Add test coverage for versioning and consent withdrawal semantics
Estimation.Complexity Reason: New privacy subsystem with write-once event semantics and downstream integration into existing business actions.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO, Redis, Riak, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: Legal policy versioning conventions, Event immutability approach and archival requirement

Estimation.Issue: GDPR Missing: Comprehensive subject-access endpoint not implemented
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Define DSAR response model (machine-readable + human-readable export), Create `DataSubjectAccessController` and DSAR request workflow, Implement `DsarAggregationService` with repository projections, Add throttling, idempotency, and async fulfillment status APIs, Persist DSAR request lifecycle and fulfillment audit records, Add e2e tests for access boundaries and anti-replay controls
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Broad aggregation and strict access assurance needed; similar complexity to portability with additional SLA/state tracking.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO, Redis, Riak, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: Shared data model decisions with portability module, Operational SLA and queueing strategy

Estimation.Issue: GDPR Missing: Centralized retention/deletion policy engine for PII
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Define retention policy model and rule schema, Implement `RetentionEngineService` with scheduled execution and dry-run mode, Add entity-specific deletion/anonymization handlers, Add execution logs, error dead-letter handling, and rerun support, Implement distributed lock/checkpointing for singleton scheduler, Backfill policy coverage tests for major PII entities
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Cross-entity destructive processing with high data-loss risk, strong idempotency requirements, and operational rollback concerns.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Service, DAO, Redis, Riak, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: Data ownership map across all PII tables/entities, Operational approval workflow for deletion policies, Backup/restore validation before rollout

Estimation.Issue: SOC2 Partial: Access control split between permitAll filter chain and custom interceptor/manual checks
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Refactor `SecurityFilterChain` to deny-by-default and explicit public allowlist, Implement `JwtAuthenticationFilter` (`OncePerRequestFilter`) and populate `SecurityContext`, Add centralized auth error handling (`AuthenticationEntryPoint`, `AccessDeniedHandler`), Remove manual bearer parsing from controllers/services, Migrate existing interceptor-only checks to Spring Security primitives, Run endpoint-by-endpoint regression security tests
Estimation.Complexity Reason: High blast radius authentication change; every endpoint behavior (401/403/public) must be revalidated.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, Security Config, Redis, DAO
Estimation.Dependencies: Complete public endpoint inventory, Coordination with RBAC standardization item

Estimation.Issue: SOC2 Partial: RBAC exists but is uneven and not centralized
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Enable method security globally and define authorization conventions, Implement custom `PermissionEvaluator` and workspace-scoped checks, Replace manual role checks with `@PreAuthorize` across critical methods, Normalize role-permission model and repository access patterns, Add permission-cache invalidation strategy and tests
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Touches many service/controller methods and requires careful compatibility with existing role semantics.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO, Redis, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: Depends on access-control refactor baseline, Role model cleanup and data seeding strategy

Estimation.Issue: SOC2 Partial: Audit logging coverage is limited to selected actions
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Define critical action catalog and audit schema, Implement `@Auditable` annotation and AOP capture layer, Create async `AuditWriter` with structured JSON and correlation IDs, Persist audit records to DB and optional immutable archive, Instrument auth/privilege/data-change flows, Add tamper-evidence/hash-chain verification tests
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Cross-cutting instrumentation with performance sensitivity and schema design requirements for long-term storage.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO, Redis, Riak, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: Final action taxonomy from security/compliance, Storage sizing and retention policy alignment

Estimation.Issue: SOC2 Missing: Redis caching controls absent because Redis integration is absent
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Add Spring Data Redis dependency and `RedisConfig`, Implement token revocation cache (`jti`) and logout invalidation, Implement login/API rate-limiting filter backed by Redis counters, Add policy/permission cache namespaces and TTL standards, Add resilience/fallback behavior when Redis is unavailable, Add integration tests against Redis test container
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Net-new infra integration but bounded scope if limited to revocation/rate-limit/policy cache primitives.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Service, Redis, Security Config, Controller
Estimation.Dependencies: Redis instance provisioning and network/security setup, Operational monitoring for cache availability

Estimation.Issue: SOC2 Missing: Riak storage controls absent because Riak integration is absent
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Introduce Riak client configuration and health integration, Implement `RiakComplianceStore` abstraction and bucket conventions, Build write/read adapters for audit/privacy artifacts, Add metadata model (owner, purpose, retention, checksum), Integrate encryption before Riak write path, Add failure/retry and archive retrieval tests
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Greenfield NoSQL integration with serialization, metadata, and reliability concerns; requires operational readiness.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Service, DAO, Riak, Redis, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: Riak cluster availability and bucket policy setup, Crypto/key management dependency for encrypted payloads

Estimation.Issue: SOC2 Missing: Centralized monitoring/tracing instrumentation not implemented
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Enable Micrometer metrics and structured telemetry baseline, Instrument auth/security counters and API latency/status metrics, Add correlation-id propagation interceptor/filter, Define alertable SLO/SLI metrics and tags, Add dashboards/alerts hooks for security events, Add load tests to validate overhead
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Implementation is straightforward but requires alignment with runtime observability stack and naming conventions.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, Security Config, Redis, Riak
Estimation.Dependencies: Monitoring backend availability (Prometheus/APM/SIEM), Alert routing ownership

Estimation.Issue: ISO27001 Partial: Encryption practice is mixed (BCrypt good, AES utility weak)
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Design `CryptoService` with AES-GCM envelope format, Integrate key-id aware encryption/decryption APIs, Replace legacy `EncryptionUtil` usages in auth/user flows, Add cryptographic test vectors and backward compatibility migration strategy, Add key rotation support hooks in crypto layer
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Security-critical refactor with backward compatibility constraints for previously encrypted values.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Service, DAO, Security Config, Riak, Redis
Estimation.Dependencies: Secret management/KMS availability, Data migration plan for old encrypted payloads

Estimation.Issue: ISO27001 Partial: Secure configuration has unsafe defaults (useSSL=false, ddl-auto=update)
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Create secure baseline config validator and fail-fast startup checks, Harden prod properties (DB TLS, actuator, CORS, SQL logging levels), Add profile-specific configuration tests, Document and enforce non-dev forbidden config values
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Mostly configuration and guardrail logic with limited code churn.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Security Config, DAO, Redis, Riak
Estimation.Dependencies: Infra cert setup for DB/Redis/Riak TLS, Deployment pipeline update for secure env vars

Estimation.Issue: ISO27001 Partial: Risk controls are selective, not codified uniformly
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Define security control baseline and annotation model (`@SensitiveOperation`), Implement enforcement interceptor/policy service, Add CI architecture/security policy checks (e.g., ArchUnit rules), Refactor high-risk endpoints/services to baseline controls, Add control conformance reporting
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Framework-level changes plus broad adoption effort across existing modules.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, Security Config, Redis, Riak
Estimation.Dependencies: Security governance ownership for policy definitions, Team adoption and code-review checklist updates

Estimation.Issue: ISO27001 Missing: Secrets management pattern not compliant (secrets in properties)
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Replace hardcoded properties with env/secret references, Implement `SecretProvider` abstraction and startup validation, Integrate Vault/KMS/secret manager client for runtime retrieval, Rotate exposed credentials and invalidate old secrets, Update deployment manifests/pipelines for secret injection
Estimation.Complexity Reason: App and infra changes are tightly coupled; credential rotation must be coordinated with zero downtime.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Security Config, Service, DAO, Redis, Riak
Estimation.Dependencies: Secret manager provisioning and IAM policies, Credential rotation windows with external providers (DB, SMTP, Stripe, etc.)

Estimation.Issue: ISO27001 Missing: No key rotation/revocation lifecycle for JWT/API secrets
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Design key registry model (`kid`, status, activation, revocation timestamps), Implement `JwtKeyProvider` supporting active/previous keys, Add token minting with `kid` and validation by key id, Implement admin/internal key revoke/rollover workflow, Add cache invalidation and propagation for revocation events, Add rotation runbook and automated rotation tests
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Authentication core behavior changes and requires safe rollout for existing active tokens.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Security Config, Service, DAO, Redis, Riak, Controller
Estimation.Dependencies: Depends on secrets/KMS integration, Client/session compatibility strategy during cutover

Estimation.Issue: ISO27001 Missing: No systematic field-level encryption for sensitive persisted data
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Identify sensitive fields across entities and repositories, Implement JPA `AttributeConverter` or encrypted-field abstraction, Apply encryption/decryption to entity persistence path, Build migration job to re-encrypt existing plaintext data, Update query/index strategy for encrypted fields, Add masking in DTO mappers and privileged-read checks
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Large data-model impact, migration complexity, query behavior changes, and high backward compatibility risk.
Estimation.Impacted Components: DAO, Service, Controller, Security Config, Redis, Riak
Estimation.Dependencies: Crypto service and key lifecycle readiness, Data migration window and rollback strategy, Performance validation for encrypted fields

Estimation.Issue: Cross-cutting Risk: Sensitive data appears in logs and exception messages
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Define log redaction policy (PII/token/session patterns), Implement centralized redaction/masking in logging pipeline, Add global exception translator with sanitized API error model, Refactor controllers to remove raw payload/exception message logging, Add tests for redaction and safe error responses
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Cross-cutting but technically straightforward; main effort is endpoint cleanup and regression validation.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, Security Config, Riak, Redis
Estimation.Dependencies: Agreement on masking rules and observability format, Coordination with audit logging implementation

Estimation.Issue: GDPR Missing: Personal data inventory and access registry is not centralized
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Define inventory schema and ownership model, Implement inventory APIs and service, Tag DTO/entity fields by data class/sensitivity, Build storage/access mapping views, Add governance reports and tests
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Cross-cutting metadata capture across many modules with governance validation dependency.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: Compliance owner approval for taxonomy, Data classification policy finalization

Estimation.Issue: GDPR Missing: Minors (under 16) consent and age-gating controls are not implemented
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Add age verification fields/validation, Implement parental consent workflow, Block processing without valid consent state, Add audit trail and expiry handling, Add integration/regression tests
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Changes user onboarding and consent states with legal-policy sensitivity and compatibility impact.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO, Security Config, Redis
Estimation.Dependencies: Legal policy for age verification, UX/frontend flow alignment

Estimation.Issue: GDPR Missing: Cookie and form consent lifecycle controls are not comprehensively implemented
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Implement consent preference APIs, Capture form-level consent evidence, Add opt-in/opt-out/withdraw endpoints, Integrate consent checks into messaging/forms, Add consent reporting and tests
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Requires coordination with frontend behavior and persistent consent history enforcement.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO, Redis, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: Frontend cookie/banner implementation alignment, Policy text/version governance

Estimation.Issue: GDPR Missing: Third-party processor and international transfer governance is not modeled
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Create processor and transfer schema, Build processor registry APIs, Capture transfer safeguards and assessments, Link processors to data flows, Add periodic review workflow
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Mostly governance data modeling with lower runtime risk but depends on policy inputs.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO
Estimation.Dependencies: Legal/compliance input for transfer basis, Vendor inventory completeness

Estimation.Issue: GDPR Missing: Breach response and 72-hour notification workflow is not implemented
Estimation.Tasks (comma separated): Design incident state machine, Implement breach incident APIs, Add timer-based 72-hour escalation logic, Build authority/user notification tracking, Create post-incident action records
Estimation.Complexity Reason: Workflow orchestration and SLA-driven operations require reliable state transitions and alerting.
Estimation.Impacted Components: Controller, Service, DAO, Redis, Security Config
Estimation.Dependencies: SOC/incident-management process integration, Notification channel ownership

Executive Summary.Details: Hardcoded secrets and credentials in config files (spring.datasource.password, jwt.secret, OpenAI keys, SMTP password, ZeptoMail key, Stripe keys) create credential exposure and compromise risk. | Authorization model risk: SecurityConfig permits all requests at filter-chain level, with enforcement moved to custom interceptor/manual checks, increasing bypass/misconfiguration risk. | Potential sensitive-data logging risk: request-associated values (e.g., imageUrl, sessionId, search criteria) and exception message propagation appear in controller logging/response paths. | Cryptographic weakness risk from EncryptionUtil hardcoded AES key and AES/ECB/PKCS5Padding usage. | Data subject rights coverage risk: deletion exists for user path, but comprehensive personal-data portability and consent lifecycle evidence is not present. | Assurance gap risk for cache/NoSQL controls because Redis/Riak control implementation is absent in code. | GDPR governance risk: missing centralized processor/international transfer controls can expose the organization to cross-border compliance violations. | Consent evidence risk: absence of structured cookie/form/privacy-policy consent logs may weaken legal defensibility during audits or complaints. | Incident response risk: without breach workflow orchestration and notification tracking, 72-hour GDPR notification obligations may be missed.
ISO27001 Partial: Secure configuration has unsafe defaults (useSSL=false, ddl-auto=update)

SOC2 Partial: Access control split between permitAll filter chain and custom interceptor/manual checks | SOC2 Partial: RBAC exists but is uneven and not centralized | ISO27001 Missing: Secrets management pattern not compliant (secrets in properties) | ISO27001 Missing: No key rotation/revocation lifecycle for JWT/API secrets | ISO27001 Partial: Encryption practice is mixed (BCrypt good, AES utility weak) | ISO27001 Missing: No systematic field-level encryption for sensitive persisted data | GDPR Missing: Centralized retention/deletion policy engine for PII | GDPR Missing: Minors (under 16) consent and age-gating controls are not implemented | GDPR Missing: Breach response and 72-hour notification workflow is not implemented
Single-team sequential: ~28-36 weeks. With 3 parallel streams (Security Core, Privacy/Data Rights, Platform/Observability): ~14-18 weeks including integration hardening.
